correctness.justification: All spec requirements implemented correctly: single @ split, local part character validation, no leading/trailing dots, no consecutive dots, domain label validation, hyphen start/end check, TLD alphabetic >= 2 chars, 254-char max length. All 24 acceptance tests pass. Clean decomposition into _validate_local and _validate_domain helpers.

edge_cases.justification: All spec edge cases covered: empty string, missing @, multiple @, empty local part, no dot in domain, single-char TLD, 254-char valid email, >254-char rejection. Also handles: empty domain labels from consecutive dots, domain labels ending with hyphens, TLD with digits. Very thorough.

code_quality.justification: Well-structured with separate validation functions for local and domain parts. Compiled regex patterns at module level (good for performance). Full type hints. Clear variable naming. Follows early-return pattern for validation. The 'if not ... return False ... return True' pattern at the end of validate_email could be simplified but is clear. 104 lines total, well within limits.

documentation.justification: Module docstring explains scope and limitations clearly. All three functions have docstrings with Args and Returns sections. The module docstring explicitly notes what is NOT supported (RFC compliance, quoted local parts, IP literals, etc.), which is valuable context.

test_coverage.justification: 21 test cases using parametrize for valid/invalid spec examples, plus individual edge case tests: multiple @, empty domain labels, domain hyphen endings, TLD with digits, max length boundary, all allowed specials in local part, disallowed chars, minimal valid email. Good use of pytest.mark.parametrize. Missing: very long local/domain parts individually, unicode input, None input.

robustness.justification: Good defensive coding: checks empty string first, validates length, splits and checks count. All validation is explicit. However, no type checking on input (passing None or int would crash with AttributeError rather than a clear error). The compiled regex patterns ensure consistent validation. The 254-char limit is a practical robustness feature.
